title: OpenAI API Engineering Masterclass
subtitle: Build production AI products with OpenAI's APIs — from first call to live system
description: Most OpenAI tutorials stop at 'here's how to call the chat endpoint.' This course is for engineers and product builders who need to ship real AI products: agents that take action, tools that work in production, evals that catch regressions, systems that don't melt under load. Grounded in OpenAI's primary documentation — what the docs actually say, what they imply, and what they don't tell you.
audience: Software engineers, technical founders, and AI product builders shipping real systems on top of OpenAI's APIs. Comfortable reading code; need a real mental model for choosing models, designing agents, and getting production-grade reliability.
prerequisites: Comfortable reading code (JS, Python, or similar)
Have made at least one HTTP request to an API before
An OpenAI API key (free tier is fine for following along)
No machine learning background required — we cover the mental model from scratch
outcomes: Explain how OpenAI's models actually work at a level that informs engineering decisions
Choose the right model and API surface for any given task
Design prompts and structured outputs that are reliable across runs
Build an agent that uses tools safely, with guardrails and approvals
Implement RAG with embeddings + vector search
Set up evals that catch regressions before they ship
Optimize for the right axis (cost vs latency vs accuracy) per use case
Take a prototype through OpenAI's production checklist

modules.title: The OpenAI Mental Model
modules.summary: What you actually need to know about how OpenAI's models work to make good engineering decisions. The API surface, the models, the tokens, the cost — everything that compounds.

lessons.title: How OpenAI's models actually work (no math required)
lessons.objective: Build a working mental model of how LLMs generate text — enough to predict their behavior in your code.

lessons.title: The API surface — Responses, Chat Completions, and what's actually different
lessons.objective: Pick the right OpenAI API for any task and understand why the surface is the way it is.

lessons.title: Models, capabilities, and how to pick the right one
lessons.objective: Choose between GPT-5 family, reasoning models, and specialized variants based on task, budget, and latency.

lessons.title: Tokens, context windows, and the things that decide your cost
lessons.objective: Predict the token cost of a request before you send it, and structure prompts to stay within budget.

modules.title: Core Capabilities
modules.summary: Prompting, structured output, function calling, embeddings, multimodal. The building blocks that show up in every real product.

lessons.title: Text generation and prompting — what actually works
lessons.objective: Write prompts that produce reliable output, using the patterns OpenAI's own prompt engineering guide endorses.

lessons.title: Structured output — making the model return JSON you can trust
lessons.objective: Use structured output and JSON schema mode to get parseable, predictable responses.

lessons.title: Function calling and tool use
lessons.objective: Wire OpenAI models up to call your own functions and external APIs, with proper error handling.

lessons.title: Embeddings and semantic search
lessons.objective: Build a working semantic search / RAG system with text-embedding-3 and a vector store.

lessons.title: Images, vision, audio, and the multimodal stack
lessons.objective: Generate images, parse documents and screenshots, transcribe audio, and synthesize speech with the OpenAI multimodal APIs.

modules.title: Agents and Tools
modules.summary: What an agent actually is, the Agents SDK, how tool use works in production, and the safety patterns you need before going live.

lessons.title: What an agent actually is (and isn't)
lessons.objective: Define the agent loop, distinguish agents from chatbots and pipelines, and recognize when an agent is the right architecture.

lessons.title: The Agents SDK — building your first one
lessons.objective: Build a working OpenAI Agent that takes a task, uses tools, and returns a structured result.

lessons.title: Tool use patterns that work in production
lessons.objective: Design tools (function schemas, error handling, retry logic) that survive real user input and edge cases.

lessons.title: MCP and Connectors — the standard for tool ecosystems
lessons.objective: Use OpenAI's MCP support to plug into existing tool servers, and decide when to build your own MCP server.

lessons.title: Guardrails, approvals, and keeping agents safe
lessons.objective: Apply OpenAI's guardrail and approval patterns to prevent your agent from taking dangerous or unintended actions.

modules.title: Production Systems
modules.summary: What separates a prototype from a live product. Conversation state, streaming, prompt caching, evals, and picking what to optimize.

lessons.title: Conversation state, streaming, and webhooks
lessons.objective: Persist conversation state across turns, stream responses for fast perceived latency, and handle async work via webhooks.

lessons.title: Prompt caching, context compaction, and the cost game
lessons.objective: Cut request cost and latency dramatically by structuring prompts for caching and compacting long histories.

lessons.title: Evals — how to know if your AI product is getting better
lessons.objective: Set up evals that catch regressions before they hit users, using OpenAI's evals tooling.

lessons.title: Latency, cost, accuracy — picking what to optimize
lessons.objective: Diagnose which dimension is hurting your product and apply the right optimization technique without making the others worse.

modules.title: Advanced Patterns and Going Live
modules.summary: Fine-tuning when it helps, reasoning models, multi-step workflows, and OpenAI's production deployment checklist.

lessons.title: Fine-tuning — when it actually helps (and when it doesn't)
lessons.objective: Decide whether fine-tuning is the right tool for your problem, and what kind (supervised, DPO, RFT) to use.

lessons.title: Reasoning models and the new 'think first' paradigm
lessons.objective: Use OpenAI's reasoning models effectively, understand reasoning_effort, and know when they're worth the cost.

lessons.title: Multi-step workflows: deep research, computer use, voice agents
lessons.objective: Build longer-horizon AI products using OpenAI's specialized capabilities: deep research, computer use, real-time voice.

lessons.title: The production checklist — going from prototype to live system
lessons.objective: Apply OpenAI's production best practices checklist to harden your AI product for real users.
